name: Point of San Jorge from Luna
name_original: Punta de San Jorge desde Luna
region_intro: Hiking around Luna offers diverse landscapes characterized by rolling hills, agricultural plains, and unique geological formations like the Aguarales de Valpalmas. The region features a mix of open terrain and areas with sparse vegetation, providing expansive views. Elevation changes are generally moderate, making many routes accessible for various fitness levels. Best hiking trails around Luna The most popular hiking route is Aguarales de Valpalmas Loop, a 6.5 miles (10.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 50 minutes to complete, offering views of distinctive eroded earth formations. Another top favourite among local hikers is Aguarales de Valpalmas from Piedratajada, a moderate 6.0 miles (9.6 km) path. This route explores similar geological features, starting from a different point. Local hikers also love the Santuario del Monlora from Luna — region of the Five Villages, a 8.4 miles (13.6 km) trail leading through varied rural landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 55 minutes. Hiking around Luna is defined by its open plains, gentle hills, and unique badlands topography, with options ranging from family friendly hikes to more challenging day hikes. The network of Luna trails offers routes for different ability levels. The routes in Luna are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 200 hikers have used komoot to explore Luna's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How long do hiking trails around Luna typically take to complete?
A: Hiking durations in Luna vary, but many popular routes can be completed within a few hours. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981939836/aguarales-de-valpalmas-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguarales de Valpalmas Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a 6.5-mile (10.5 km) trail that takes approximately 2 hours 50 minutes. A slightly longer option, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981979326/santuario-del-monlora-from-luna-region-of-the-five-villages" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santuario del Monlora from Luna&lt;/a&gt;, covers 8.4 miles (13.6 km) and typically takes around 3 hours 55 minutes.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Luna?
A: Yes, Luna offers routes suitable for families. The region's moderate elevation changes and diverse terrain include options that are accessible for various fitness levels, making them enjoyable for all ages. While specific 'easy' routes are fewer, many of the moderate trails can be adapted for a family pace.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Luna?
A: Absolutely. Circular routes are a great way to experience the diverse landscapes of Luna. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981939836/aguarales-de-valpalmas-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguarales de Valpalmas Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which allows you to explore the unique eroded earth formations and return to your starting point without retracing your steps. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981894150/collado-de-cherre-circular-by-the-sierra-de-las-pedrosas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado de Cherre — circular by the Sierra de las Pedrosas&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What unique geological features can I see while hiking in Luna?
A: Luna is known for its distinctive geological formations, particularly the Aguarales de Valpalmas, which are eroded earth formations resembling miniature canyons or badlands. You can experience these unique landscapes on trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981939836/aguarales-de-valpalmas-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguarales de Valpalmas Loop&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e947206102/aguarales-de-valpalmas-from-piedratajada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguarales de Valpalmas from Piedratajada&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any challenging day hikes in Luna?
A: While most trails in Luna are rated as moderate, offering a good workout without extreme difficulty, the region does provide options for longer, more demanding day hikes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981979326/santuario-del-monlora-from-luna-region-of-the-five-villages" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santuario del Monlora from Luna&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, is an 8.4-mile (13.6 km) route with a significant elevation gain of over 370 meters, providing a more strenuous experience.

Q: What kind of views can I expect on hikes around Luna?
A: Hikes around Luna offer expansive views across rolling hills and agricultural plains. The open terrain and sparse vegetation provide clear sightlines, especially in areas with unique geological formations like the Aguarales de Valpalmas. You'll often find panoramic vistas of the surrounding rural landscapes.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Luna?
A: The trails in Luna are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the unique badlands topography, the open plains, and the gentle hills, highlighting the diverse and accessible nature of the routes.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or attractions near the hiking trails in Luna?
A: Yes, the area around Luna offers several points of interest. You can explore natural monuments such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2703458" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mallos de Agüero&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/558941" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguarales de Valdemilaz&lt;/a&gt;. Other attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5337388" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santa María de la Corona&lt;/a&gt; in Ejea de los Caballeros, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5857443" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ardisa Dam&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there options for beginner hikers in Luna?
A: Yes, Luna has options suitable for beginner hikers. While most routes are classified as moderate, the overall elevation changes are generally gentle, making many trails accessible for those new to hiking or looking for a less strenuous walk. It's always recommended to check the specific route details for distance and elevation before heading out.

Q: Is winter hiking possible in Luna?
A: Winter hiking in Luna is generally possible, as the region typically experiences mild winters compared to more mountainous areas. However, conditions can vary, so it's advisable to check local weather forecasts for ice or snow, especially in shaded areas or after precipitation. Dress in layers and wear appropriate footwear.

Q: What should I wear for hiking in Luna?
A: Given Luna's open terrain and varied weather, layering is key. Wear comfortable, moisture-wicking clothing, and bring a waterproof or windproof outer layer. Sturdy hiking shoes with good grip are essential, especially when traversing the unique geological formations. Don't forget sun protection like a hat and sunscreen, as many areas have sparse vegetation.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Luna?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many hiking trails in Luna, especially in rural and natural areas. However, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially near agricultural land or wildlife. Please be mindful of local regulations and ensure you clean up after your pet to maintain the natural environment for everyone.
tips: Nice church located in the town of Luna. It is Romanesque in style and we will find it near the Torre Palacio del Reloj de la Corona from the 11th century, built as a watchtower for the lands reconquered from the Moors.
Viewpoint that we find on the ascent to Punta de San Jorge with good views of Monlore and on clear days of Moncayo and the western peaks of the Pyrenees

We are at the Punta de San Jorge that we access from the town of Luna by a friendly path. Upstairs we will find an esplanade and picnic area where you can sit and recover energy in the shade with beautiful views of Moncayo to the south and the main western peaks of the Pyrenees to the northeast

waypoints.name: Viewpoint of Punta de San Jorge
